channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Los Angeles Heat Builds Ahead of Hot Spell Across US Southwest
item.description: Temperatures are set to rise across Los Angeles and parts of the southwestern US later this week.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: EU Aims to Cut Dependency on Soy, Oilseed Imports for Livestock
item.description: The European Union aims to increase the amount of protein feed crops, such as soybeans, that are sourced from within the 27-nation bloc, cutting its dependency on top suppliers like the US and Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Max Ramsay

item.title: London’s Hot Summer Prompts JPMorgan to Ease Strict WFH Rules
item.description: Some of the UK capital’s toughest return-to-office policies have been relaxed amid record temperatures
item.dc:creator: Nil Codina Martinez, Olivia Rudgard, Claudia Cohen, Arno Schuetze, Todd Woody

item.title: Geothermal Startup Raises $134 Million for Drilling Rocks
item.description: Geothermal startup Quaise Energy raised $134 million to develop technology for drilling superhot rocks.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Ma

item.title: Germany Recorded 5,000 Excess Deaths in Late-June Heat Wave
item.description: Germany recorded more than 5,000 excess deaths during the late-June heat wave, according to preliminary data from the Federal Statistical Office.
item.dc:creator: Eva Brendel

item.title: JPMorgan Relaxes Return-to-Office Rules for London Heat Wave
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has relaxed some of the finance industry’s strictest return-to-office requirements for its London staff, after the UK capital sweltered through record-high temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Nil Codina Martinez, Olivia Rudgard, Claudia Cohen, Arno Schuetze

item.title: Faster, Fiercer Wildfires Are Testing Evacuation Plans
item.description: As heat waves fuel more intense blazes, fire officials are rethinking how to safely empty hard-to-escape neighborhoods
item.dc:creator: Todd Woody

item.title: Germany Plans to Cut €2.1 Billion in Heat Pump Subsidies by 2030
item.description: Germany plans to cut generous subsidies for low-carbon heat pumps by about €2.1 billion ($2.4 billion) in the coming years, as it aims to make some budget savings while raising spending on defense and security.
item.dc:creator: Petra Sorge

item.title: ECB Rolls Out Haircuts on Collateral as Climate Risks Grow
item.description: The European Central Bank is imposing additional so-called haircuts on collateral that poses a climate risk, to guard against possible losses.
item.dc:creator: Frances Schwartzkopff

item.title: Mumbai Struggles to Cope as Record Rains Lash Financial Hub
item.description: Mumbai’s wettest start to July in decades killed at least 13 people in the city and its adjoining areas and caused widespread disruption for millions of residents, raising questions about the quality of infrastructure in India’s financial hub.
item.dc:creator: Anup Roy, Azman Usmani
